data.title: Pi Network’s New Migration Roadmap: Lags Timeline, Dates, and Exact Criteria.
data.description:
The post Pi Network’s New Migration Roadmap: Lags Timeline, Dates, and Exact Criteria. The roadmap outlines a three-phase plan to move millions of users, or “Pioneers,” to the open network.   But here’s the catch—there’s no timeline, no dates, and still a lot of uncertainty
source: https://coinpedia.org/news/pi-networks-new-migration-roadmap-lags-timeline-dates-and-exact-criteria/

data.title: Lyn Alden maintains $85K Bitcoin target despite Trump tariffs, cites strong institutional demand
data.description:
19-4-2025 – Macroeconomist Lyn Alden has tempered her outlook for Bitcoin’s price trajectory in light of U. S. President Donald Trump’s recent tariff declarations, forecasting a modest downward tweak. Nevertheless, her confidence in the cryptocurrency’s ascent remains unshaken, projecting that Bitcoin will surpass $85,000 by the close of 2025
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/lyn-alden-maintains-85k-bitcoin-target-despite-trump-tariffs-cites-strong-institutional-demand/

data.title: Vietnam’s Ministry of Finance partners with Bybit to launch crypto trading pilot project, establish legal framework
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the Vietnamese Ministry of Finance announced that it will cooperate with the cryptocurrency exchange Bybit to launch a cryptocurrency trading pilot project and is committed to establishing a legal framework for the country's large group of crypto users. This move marks an important step forward in Vietnam's cryptocurrency regulation. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 85,000 USDT with a 0.39% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 19, 2025, 04:29 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 85,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 85,019.460938 USDT, with a narrowed 0.39% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitcoin's Year-End Price Forecast Remains Optimistic Despite Tariff Announcement
data.description: According to Odaily, macroeconomist Lyn Alden has noted a slight downward adjustment in Bitcoin's forecasted price following U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of tariffs. Despite this, Alden anticipates that Bitcoin's price will exceed $85,000 by the end of 2025. She suggests that if there is a significant liquidity release, Bitcoin could reach even more optimistic targets, reminiscent of the global economic cycle from 2003 to 2007.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin's year-end forecast lowered to $85,000 after "tariff turmoil"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Macroeconomist Lyn Alden said that after President Trump announced the tariffs, the predicted price of Bitcoin was slightly lowered, but it is expected that the price of Bitcoin will still be higher than the current $85,000 at the end of 2025. Alden believes that if a "huge liquidity release" occurs, Bitcoin may reach a more optimistic target, similar to the global economic cycle from 2003 to 2007. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin Mega Whales Keep Buying—Is Rest Of Market Finally Catching Up?
data.description:
On-chain data shows the largest of Bitcoin investors have continued to buy recently. Here’s whether the other cohorts have followed in the footsteps of these titans or not. Mid-Sized Bitcoin Holders May Finally Be Showing A Shift In a new post on X, the on-chain analytics firm Glassnode has discussed about the how the Accumulation Trend Score has looked for the various cohorts in the Bitcoin market. The “Accumulation Trend Score” is an indicator that tells us about whether the Bitcoin investors are participating in buying or selling
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-mega-whales-keep-buying-market-finally/
